human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for hospitality-operations-specific execution behavior, healthcare administrative boundary discipline, prerequisite validation, lifecycle state progression, PHI and patient-safety escalation, payer-policy refusal, completion evidence, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

review_notes: All required scenario families for the healthcare-and-care-delivery research contract are covered and mapped to passing results.
All required functional abilities for shared baseline, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and healthcare-and-care-delivery are represented and validated.
The lane remains specification-based and does not claim a retained implementation.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls restrict the lane to hospitality and travel execution, readiness, exception handling, completion evidence, and healthcare-administrative prerequisite checking without clinical, payer, privacy, financial, legal, fraud, or safety authority drift.

Authority sources cover HHS/OCR HIPAA minimum-necessary guidance, CMS managed-care and discharge-planning guidance, HL7 FHIR workflow, DOJ ADA lodging rules, FTC lodging fee guidance, DOT travel guidance, DOT refund guidance, and PCI SSC payment standards with refresh and decay rules.

Scenario set covers accepted work to completion, missing authorization/referral/eligibility, incomplete mid-process artifacts, execution rework, no-evidence closure refusal, clinical judgment refusal, PHI handling, payer interpretation refusal, realistic terminology, financial boundary rejection, low-confidence escalation, conflicting policy escalation, and tenant adaptation.
Runtime remains specification-based with explicit promotion requirements for any later retained-implementation transition.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; the hospitality-and-travel path plus healthcare-and-care-delivery resolver ambiguity is non-blocking because it is explicitly represented in the research summary and constrained by runtime retrieval rules.
This package is ready for validated operations under specification-based execution controls.
